title: Can someone explain this apparent plot hole please
display_title: Can someone explain this apparent plot hole please
wikitext:
[]: Can someone explain this (apparent) plot hole please?             Quote Post by endlessly » Tue Sep 15, 2015 5:28 am I use the qualifier "(apparent)" because I know I am someone easily overwhelmed when lots of information comes out at once, and I assume there is probably a straight forward answer somewhere since I've never seen anyone else bring this up and it seems to be a pretty big plot hole if it is correct. My issue is that I don't understand why NERV and SEELE would try to stop the angels coming into contact with Lilith and Adam even though they both want to enact the human instrumentality project? I've seen explanations that the angels coming into contact with either Adam or Lilith would result in third impact, which would destroy Lilith and all its descendants, and that would allow the Earth to be then ruled by the angels. However, NERV and SEELE apparently believe Third Impact is necessary for the Human Instrumentality project, which they both want, so what is the difference here? What determines that one third impact will be for the benefit of NERV and SEELE by uniting humanity and what determines that another third impact will destroy humanity and allow the angels to rule the planet? And on the topic of this, what parts of Adam and Lilith combining result in which event exactly? Gendo wanted to combine Rei, Lilith's soul, with Adam's body. However, Rei rejects him and combines herself, Lilith's soul, with Adam's body and Lilith's body. Can someone explain why Lilith's soul with Adam's body would create a 'good' third impact that Gendo wanted while a third impact created from Adam's body and Lilith's soul and body would be a 'bad' third impact? Is it something about the soul not being able to combine with its body? Is that what would result in a 'bad' third impact? If so, why? And is the combination of Adam's body with Lilith's soul, or even Lilith's body with Adam's soul, meant to be a 'good' third impact? Again, if so, why? As you can probably tell I'm really confused. I've been looking for explanations and I often use the evageeks wiki, so I was hoping someone could be kind enough to explain these things to me. I love the series, and I'm rewatching it so as to full appreciate it, since I first watched it two years ago when all anime was to me was action and gore and I couldnt properly appreciate what NGE had to offer. I'm really inpatient and currently working on a lot of assignments, so I don't really have time to watch the series, but these questions have just been annoying me for so long that I'm desperate for answers. I'd also like to finally finish the series with complete knowledge of the deal with the Adam, Lilith, NERV, and SEELE, since it will then just be easier to understand what's going on. Oh and if you give me any answers could you please provide sources (directly from the anime series/movie if possible) just so I know where to go for direct quotes and answers. Thanks in advance to anyone who can help me :)

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Tue Sep 15, 2015 6:45 am I've always thought it was to do with control. If an Angel comes into contact with Adam/Lilith and causes the Third Impact then it wipes out humanity completely with no instrumentality/rebirth happening or at least it'll happen but SEELE/Gendo won't have the appropriate control over it that they crave. When SEELE try to initiate it they are trying to put something they control at dead centre so that everything will go according to their plans. They can do what they want to do exactly as they wish to do so. Same with Gendo but of course the plans are scuppered when Shinji is instead given the keys to the machine so to speak. Fyutsuki kinda alludes to this in EoE when Shinji is taken up, how the fate of humanity rests on his shoulders. (I can't remember the exact quote, I'm at work at the moment so a little bit limited on what I can look up) Someone can probably explain this more eloquently than me but I do think the simplest answer is that if an Angel makes contact it means SEELE can't control anything so they need them out of the way.

[]:             Quote Post by Adaminator1 » Tue Sep 15, 2015 7:11 am Yeah. They both want to make sure it happens in different ways. It's a matter of controlling the outcome.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 15, 2015 1:57 pm They both want to start 3I (the Angels actually want to reunite with Adam, not Lilith, but they can't tell the two apart), but only Seele want HIP. Not the same thing.
